chore(beads): update issue tracking for membrane monitor expansion

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-19h","title":"Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (integrate into barrier/admissibility)","description":"Wire SOS barrier evaluation into RuntimeMathKernel decision law.

Plan:
- Feed barrier evaluator with state extracted from cached monitors (risk bound, quarantine depth, contention, etc.).
- If barrier indicates unsafe region: force FullValidate and/or Repair/Deny depending on mode.
- Export barrier margin in snapshot for observability.

Acceptance criteria:
- In strict mode, barrier only escalates (never silently repairs semantics).
- In hardened mode, barrier can trigger repair actions per policy.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.415903108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.436433233Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19h","depends_on_id":"bd-19r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.360566374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19h","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.436390423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-19r","title":"Kernel: SOS barrier certificates (runtime polynomial evaluator)","description":"Implement runtime evaluator for SOS barrier polynomials.

Requirements:
- Fixed-point coefficients and inputs.
- Horner-style evaluation; constant-time wrt degree.
- No allocations; minimal branching.

Deliverables:
- sos_barrier.rs (or barrier_cert.rs) + tests for numeric stability on representative ranges.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:26.230148859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.281594985Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-19r","depends_on_id":"bd-2pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.204426981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-19r","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.281558416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1az","title":"Drift: RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema versioning + stability policy","description":"Make snapshot schema stable and future-proof.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add an explicit schema version field.
- Policy: additive-only changes; removals require version bump and migration plan.
- Document each snapshot field: what it means, units, expected range.

Rationale:
- Snapshots are fixtures for regression detection and harness diffs.","notes":"Implemented RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema stability: added schema version field + constant, documented additive-only policy, and generated a complete field schema doc.

Key files:
- crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs (schema_version + RUNTIME_KERNEL_SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION)
- crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/runtime_kernel_snapshot_schema.md (116 fields: meaning/units/range)
- crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/snapshot_diff.rs (include schema_version in key diff fields)
- tests/runtime_math/golden/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json + tests/runtime_math/golden/sha256sums.txt updated via scripts/update_golden_snapshots.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:14.020301546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:57:04.971345778Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:57:04.971273723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1es","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Deterministic repair symbol generation (overhead model + R derivation)","description":"Implement or specify deterministic repair symbol generation for evidence objects.

Spec-derived formula to adopt:
- slack_decode = 2 (target K+2 decode slack)
- R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))
- loss_fraction_max approx (R - slack_decode)/(K_source + R)

Constraints:
- Generation MUST NOT run per call; run on epoch boundary/cadence.
- Seed must be deterministic from epoch/object id.

Deliverables:
- A minimal encoder interface and test vectors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.148303508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.818822469Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-3a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.818784628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.739481467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1es","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Deterministic repair symbol generation (overhead model + R derivation)","description":"Implement or specify deterministic repair symbol generation for evidence objects.

Spec-derived formula to adopt:
- slack_decode = 2 (target K+2 decode slack)
- R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))
- loss_fraction_max approx (R - slack_decode)/(K_source + R)

Constraints:
- Generation MUST NOT run per call; run on epoch boundary/cadence.
- Seed must be deterministic from epoch/object id.

Deliverables:
- A minimal encoder interface and test vectors.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayPond","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.148303508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:59:42.387480877Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-3a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.818784628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1es","depends_on_id":"bd-kom","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:09.739481467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1h8","title":"EPIC: Harness integration for runtime evidence (asupersync + frankentui)","description":"Goal: make runtime_math and membrane behavior provable and debuggable via deterministic harness outputs.

Requirements (AGENTS.md):
- Use /dp/asupersync for deterministic orchestration + traceability primitives.
- Use /dp/frankentui for deterministic diff/snapshot reporting and TUI analysis.

Outputs:
- Harness commands that capture runtime_math snapshots and evidence ledgers.
- Deterministic diffs for regressions (repair rate, risk bounds, regret, etc.).
- Optional offline decode of erasure-coded evidence (if RaptorQ evidence is enabled).

Non-goal:
- Introducing these crates as runtime libc dependencies.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.629931340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:09.826729618Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-215","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.480993365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.402168239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.320562139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.242428998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-5ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:50.560385392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1h8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Harness Integration (asupersync + frankentui)

### Why This Exists
Runtime math and membrane healing are only valuable if we can:
- reproduce decisions deterministically
- compare behavior vs host glibc and vs our own previous versions
- explain why a decision changed (evidence ledger)

### Hard Constraint
`/dp/asupersync` and `/dp/frankentui` are **tooling only** for glibc_rust. libc runtime must not link them.

### asupersync Role
- deterministic orchestration of conformance scenarios
- traceability primitives and stable virtual time
- scripted injection of corruption/loss patterns for evidence/FEC tests

### frankentui Role
- snapshot/diff oriented output for:
  - RuntimeKernelSnapshot
  - evidence ledger entries
  - raptorq-style decode proofs

### Artifact Contracts (Make It Mechanical)
- `RuntimeKernelSnapshot` capture format must be stable + versioned.
- Harness must be able to:
  - run scenario -> capture snapshot -> diff vs golden -> emit report
  - decode evidence stream offline -> verify -> emit proof + diff

### Definition Of Done
- One-command harness entrypoint produces deterministic report.
- Snapshot schema changes are deliberate (versioned) and show up in diffs.
- Evidence decode/proof output is readable and debuggable.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hq","title":"Kernel: Groebner normal form for violation signatures (design)","description":"Design Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization for anomaly/violation signatures.

Goal:
- Produce stable root-cause IDs from many overlapping anomaly signals.
- Avoid double-counting and improve sparse latent-cause recovery.

Design tasks:
- Define signature variables (small integer features derived from cached states).
- Define constraints/relations and what the normal form represents.
- Choose offline artifact: Groebner basis + reduction table (no heavy algebra at runtime).

Acceptance criteria:
- Concrete signature schema + deterministic reduction procedure + output ID format.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:55.351807261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:55.351807261Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1om","title":"Perf: Golden snapshot outputs + sha256 regression gate","description":"Capture golden outputs for runtime_math telemetry so behavior drift is detectable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Produce one or more canonical snapshot JSONs under tests/ (or harness output fixtures).
- Store sha256 checksums; verify in CI/local gate.
- Golden set covers strict and hardened modes.

Rationale:
- We need isomorphism proofs for behavior changes; golden outputs are the cheap continuous check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.662031099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29.172839801Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29.172817930Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-1om","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Implemented golden runtime_math snapshot fixtures + sha256 regression gate.

- Added canonical golden snapshot fixture: `tests/runtime_math/golden/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json` (covers strict + hardened via mode=both).
- Added checksum file: `tests/runtime_math/golden/sha256sums.txt` (format compatible with `sha256sum -c`).
- Added gate script: `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh`
  - Regenerates snapshot into `target/runtime_math_golden/` and validates against committed sha256sums.
  - On mismatch, prints a truncated diff vs the golden fixture.
- Added update script: `scripts/update_golden_snapshots.sh` to intentionally refresh the committed golden + sha256sums when behavior changes with an isomorphism proof.
- Wired into CI: `scripts/ci.sh` now runs `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh`.

Verification:
- `scripts/snapshot_gate.sh` passes locally (stable output / sha256 match).","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:46:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-dfe","title":"EPIC: Inventory + drift control (AGENTS.md vs runtime_math code; snapshot schema stability)","description":"Goal: prevent architectural drift.

Why:
- runtime_math has many moving parts; if AGENTS.md lists required modules/decision law but code diverges, future work becomes inconsistent.

Outputs:
- A repeatable check that module inventory + decision law in AGENTS.md matches code.
- A stable, versioned RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema (new fields are additive and documented).
- Fusion signal vector size always matches number of cached states.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.669320398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:07.068091955Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1az","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:21.023572309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:20.863879595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:21.181672643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-29r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:07.068040919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-31i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:21.101953824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-ju7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:06.984247504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-p5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:36:20.942588193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesn’t silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: “why did runtime choose FullValidate here?” with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module can’t land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gn9","title":"Kernel Infra: Fixed-point units + shared math utils (ppm/milli)","description":"Establish shared numeric conventions for all runtime_math controllers.

Requirements:
- Prefer fixed-point integers over floats on hot path.
- Standardize units: ppm (0..1_000_000), milli-units for regret/wealth, ns for latency.
- Provide helper functions for saturating arithmetic and normalization.

Acceptance criteria:
- Documented conventions used consistently by new kernels (alpha-investing, SOS barrier eval, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol scheduler, proof-carrying policy).
- Bench shows no regression vs ad-hoc math.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryMeadow","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:31:47.724274442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:37.748436995Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-gn9","author":"IvoryMeadow","text":"Claimed by IvoryMeadow. Starting investigation of runtime_math numeric conventions and shared fixed-point utility opportunities (ppm/milli/ns) before targeted implementation and tests.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:29:21Z"},{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-gn9","author":"IvoryMeadow","text":"Investigation complete: runtime_math currently has pre-existing uncommitted edits and newly added modules in the exact scope of bd-gn9 (mod.rs, fusion.rs, serre_spectral.rs + new runtime_math files). Pausing overlapping edits pending maintainer direction to avoid trampling concurrent work.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:32:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ju7","title":"Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)","description":"Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:06.352984341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ju7","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:04:06.434884262Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"bd-ju7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-l2r","title":"Kernel: Alpha-Investing FDR (tests + perf evidence)","description":"Prove correctness and measure overhead for alpha-investing integration.

Tests:
- Simulated null stream: verify empirical false discovery is controlled (within expected bounds).
- Optional stopping simulation: ensure controller remains conservative.
- Regression tests for deterministic outcomes.

Perf:
- Microbench decide/observe deltas in strict/hardened.
- Confirm no exp/ln/matrix solve appears on strict hot path.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:08.403251183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:16.973883150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l2r","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:16.973847102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l2r","depends_on_id":"bd-3kz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.052242689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l2r","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:06.128779833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-p5i","title":"Drift: Validate decision-law vs decide() implementation (conservative merge rules)","description":"Ensure the documented decision law matches the code.

Acceptance criteria:
- Document the actual merge order and hard gates in decide().
- Add an invariant test: hard gates (barrier, full_validation_trigger_ppm, repair_trigger_ppm) always dominate soft heuristics.
